title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Type-System Trace: Type Complexity Restraint
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts type complexity restraint increment in a behavior-and-type regression suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.
Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
